title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body: Hey y’all! this vid has been getting a decent amount of views recently so I just wanted to say that yes, my opinion has slightly changed on a few of these topics over the past year after seeing them both with minimal Covid restrictions again. but for the most part, I think most of what I said still stands true. I also have some more experience with some factors that I didn’t mention (ie off campus housing) now, so if you have any questions about anything, feel free to ask and I’ll respond with my updated thoughts!!
author: @ShivNC

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body:
In the 1960s, the state of NC did a very smart thing by assigning major programs of study to the two schools so that that they wouldn't be in competition with each other for funding. This has allowed both universities to pursue their own paths to excellence. I think it's important to understand that NC State is North Carolina's original land grant university with its mission responding to the industrial revolution. This shaped its future toward more practical degree programs compared to classical liberal arts.
Also, NC State wins for ice cream. Howling Cow is legit and it's as fresh and locally sourced as you are going to get. The cows are just a few miles south of campus on a research "farm".
author: @jlpack62

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body: Hey. I just moved to Raleigh and I’m considering finishing my degree here after I gain residency in a year. Thanks for making this!

author: @ayushanturkar5137

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body: One should apply to a department not a school. UNC has some better departments and NCSU has some that are better or different - usually more applied or specialized in Raleigh. I applied for UNC Botany (from a Cornell B.S.) only to realize later NCSU"s Top 5 Horticultural Science Dept. was a better fit for my goals. Both schools partner in some areas like Biomedical Engineering. Also talk to profs who may become your advisors. If you find 2-3 profs in a department that align with your interests and goals, that is probably a good fit. If you can't even find one named, world-class prof then go elsewhere.
author: @LarryHatch

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body: Hey! Awesome video! Both of these schools are in my top 3... I'm a hs senior who is trying to decide where to go, and it has been super tough to compare the two because of Covid. This helped me out a lot! Thank you!
author: @laurenw460

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body: Overall amazingly fair and your familiarity with both in terms of real visits is important and adds credibility. Your sensibilities are "spot on" as the British would say. UNC should win for libraries, 7 million volumes vs. 4.3 million for State. I'm an NCSU Masters grad so trying to be objective and factual here. Hunt is amazing for technology but volumes teach, not tech. A BookBot doesn't help your research but it does save time.
author: @LarryHatch

title: UNC CHAPEL HILL vs NC STATE UNIVERSITY -WHICH IS BETTER? (comparing campuses, dorms, transport, etc)
body:
Hi! This is such a great video! I am extremely torn between the two and if you could answer some of my questions that would be so helpful! 1. I thought NCSU was better for business (I want to do marketing) because of their many offered programs, Raleigh’s opportunities, and entrepreneurship support. Plus, as I understand, UNC has very few undergrad business degrees, and if you don’t get into Kenyan Flagler your sophomore year, than is it worth risking that as a business major? 2. You might not have experienced this if you graduated before end of 2020 and then all through 2021, but I’ve heard, and read, about many cases of UNC having a toxic environment regarding respecting people’s political opinions…. This is something few talk about but it’s been a big factor in my decision, because it seems to be that UNC’s both students and definitely professors, can be quite stern and biased regarding reading or hearing opposing views… even failing students who write their honest opinions… 3. How important is the prestige at UNC if you want to go the entrepreneurial route, meaning it won’t be a huge factor having UNC on your resume?
Hope you can answer my questions! Thank you.
author: @tayamishin

title: The Unofficial NC State Complete Campus Tour - Main and Centennial
body:
A few places I didn’t get to in the video that I think are important:

-Frat Court: pretty self explanatory, a little bit off campus on Western Blvd/Varsity Dr. Looks really nice

-SAS Hall/Park Shops: class buildings on Stinson Dr (underneath the Court of NC), mainly for statistics

-Wolf Village: student apartments that freshmen are allowed to stay in, usually 4bed/2bath units. On the very west of campus.

-PNC Arena/Carter Finley Stadium: obviously where football and basketball teams play. About a 10 minute drive from campus on Trinity Rd
author: @ShivNC

title: The Unofficial NC State Complete Campus Tour - Main and Centennial
body: It's funny to think that my old dorm is now one of the honors dorms. I just lived there because it was close to the Design School.
author: @jpack85

title: The Unofficial NC State Complete Campus Tour - Main and Centennial
body: Awesome, enjoyed it. Fitts-Woolard Hall has a nice backstory about it's donors. Ed Wollard (an NCSU grad) was Chairman of the Board at Apple when they were failing. He decided he needed to save the company. and was looking for the right CEO. He put on his hat of contrition and called up Steve Jobs and begged him to come back. Jobs had some terms like fire most everyone. Wollard got the Board to do that and the rest is iPhone history. Had Woolard not made that phone call and got the Board to go along with Job's demands....
author: @LarryHatch

title: The Unofficial NC State Complete Campus Tour - Main and Centennial
body:
🎯 Key points for quick navigation:

00:23 NC State's campus on Hillsboro Street features student apartments like Valentine Commons and Nelson Hall, home to the Poole College of Management.
01:19 Surrounding the grass field are science-related buildings like Fox and David Clarke, primarily housing labs and research facilities.
02:46 The Brickyard is a central campus area adjacent to Hill Library, a key spot for studying and dining, including options like Chick-fil-A and pizza.
06:06 The Court of North Carolina is a scenic grass field surrounded by academic buildings like Poe, Harrelson, and Caldwell Hall.
09:22 Tally Student Union and Carmichael Gym are major hubs on campus offering dining, recreational facilities, and student services.
15:33 Centennial Campus hosts engineering and textile facilities, including buildings like Hunt Library and Fitzwillard Hall, known for advanced study spaces and labs.
20:37 Main library at NC State emphasizes automated book retrieval over traditional stacks.
21:08 Textiles complex houses key resources for popular textiles major, including classrooms and a scenic view.
21:48 MRC building serves as a major research hub for professors and grad students at NC State.

Made with HARPA AI

body:
This is my dream school! Id say Im on a good track to getting in so far (Im in middle school so idk) but I’m gonna drop some of my stats and can some of yall who went here tell me?

-In state
-All A’s at an advanced charter school
-Taking high school classes as a 7th grader
-volleyball captain
-involved in Theatre, Volleyball, Creative Writing, Beta Club secretary, student council VP,
- Volunteer work at hurricane relief center, daycare, summer camp for children with special needs where I was 1:1 with a camper, VBS teacher, and Meals on Wheels

I am a white girl with an upper middle class family

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was in psych lock down 2 weeks ago:  no phone or computer, no fresh air, no sunshine, no therapy offered, I felt like I came out worse than I went in, and I was suicidal.  Things MUST change.  Our society is failing us.
author: @zengwenyoga2894

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: To Rob…I remember first hand the feeling of visiting my wife in the psych ward and then going home to two young kids. In a couple of weeks, we celebrate 48 years. Hang tough, man. She’s an impressive lady.
author: @rcp2212

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: It is shocking how different psych wards around the world are. I'm in Germany and here you are not supposed to be all alone in your room. You get a routine to follow. And this routine contains the gym, group therapy, single therapy, arts, meditation and other activities. There is a lot of talking between patients and the staff. Its like a controlled holiday.
author: @larakorrat

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I have been admitted 3 times to a phych ward  some of the nicest most supportive people i ever met were patients
author: @MissMadiGD

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: How difficult and painful it is to live with mental disorders. Those who do not have them will never understand what hell it is.
author: @purpur7187

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: You're very brave to put yourself out there when you're in such a vulnerable position. Be proud of that, not many people could do it.
author: @ronr6450

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: She's so full of life and very articulate. Schizophrenia is such a cruel disease. I wish her nothing but the best.😊
author: @mike4619

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I've been in a psych ward over ten times during my 39 years of life. I'm haven't been in a psych ward since April of 2007. Which is awesome and I'm so proud of myself for that.
author: @Bubbles1984

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Im Nurse who worked on Psychic Ward for year s. You see so many cases from Drug Addiction, Mental illness so much more. God BLESS your Journey
author: @melanieeggers9667

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
My mom was in psych wards multiple times. The last time, I warned the nurses that she was lying and just putting on a face of someone that was “fine” and was there for no reason. I told them to not listen to anything she was saying and the nurses said that they would tell the doctors. She was out and back home two day later.. Exactly ONE week later, she committed suicide!
My mom was always the person that everyone wanted to be around. She was beautiful, kind, intelligent, and the life of the party. NO ONE expected her to do what she did EXCEPT her kids. My brothers and I seen this side of her every day. It was very hard listening to the side that everyone else got because she had been saying for five years that this was what she was going to do😢
Miss you mom❤
author: @squirrelsquad3.0

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
I am in a YouTube rabbit hole right now. I do not experience anything remotely like what you are living with. I admire the hell out of how you have chosen to beautify and help our world. You are a strong and courageous woman.
Thank you.
author: @Razedot

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This is destroying me! For the last 3 years my 62 year old husband of 35 years had a psychotic break. It reached a point where he is committed by the state. He was starving himself. I know he’s sitting there everyday, just sitting. Keep running little girl, keep fighting, the people who love you need you to fight.
author: @Radar2727

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
this is a freaking 5 star hotel.....the place I went...you can not go outside.... cant have phones, cant talk to the other sex, also we could not even be alone any where....wow. I know this is a year old, but you are a strong person and a beautiful soul.

Edit* Thank you for all the likes.
author: @dalekkgames5430

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was held for 5 days involuntarily after visiting the ER for panic attacks and horrible anxiety I've battled my whole life. Never once saw a doctor and never recieved a single medication including the ones I was prescribed. No clothing, no phones, no outdoor time, no visitors, and the worst part was I just had oral surgery and had lost all my teeth due to a bad infection in my jaw bone which needed a bone graft so I was unable to eat because they had no accommodation for that diet. Let's just say that's a birthday I will never forget. When asking for meds, the nurse pulled my file and there was an intake sheet with a picture of a 14 year old female in it ( I am a 32 year old male). They couldnt even handle the correct documents to assist in any sort of help.  Still to this day I have ptsd due to my stay.
author: @TheOneTheOnlyKJ

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
When I was in my late 20s I admitted myself into a psych hospital for suicidal thoughts and because I was experiencing things in my life that I did not know how to handle.
At the time, the hospital was sort of a safe haven for me. At the time it’s what I needed for support of what I was going through.
Sometimes people just need that hospital for things they’re going through.
Wishing you the best.
author: @crystalclear6660

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I'm an american, but I live in the UK. I was in the psyc ward for 3 months in full-blown psychosis. They were really good to me & the patients were great. I didn't eat for the first month but the food was actually really good. I was on a 5150 in my teens and the psyc ward in the states...it was the most lonely depressing time of my life. The conditions make a huge difference.
author: @GinaRossana-hl6ph

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I've been committed about 15 times. I've finally found a balance with therapy and medication. It's been hell, but I keep chugging along.
author: @mr.bipolar3344

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Last week I went to the ER and someone was having a some sort of psychiatric break. She was clearly terrified and screaming. Seeing the way staff treated her was heartbreaking. I kept hearing her screaming “get off of me” and hearing the nurses giggling about “the pipes on that chick” and “it’s so and sos favorite patient”. It was very disheartening.
author: @meaganhiller3029

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: It always shocks me how different psych wards can be in different places. I was admitted to a psychiatric institution involuntarily and was there for 2 weeks. I had a roommate, we weren't allowed outside of our room from 9pm-7am and weren't allowed back in our room from 7am-7pm. Eating breakfast, lunch, and dinner was mandatory and we all had to be present in the cafeteria, no one was allowed to stay back. We had to attend group sessions to talk about battling mental illness and coping mechanisms. It wasn't very effective. We had a TV in the common area, but they wouldn't turn it on unless it was to watch a movie. I spent all of my free time reading and coloring. We weren't allowed to go outside and only had one window in the whole ward. It literally felt like prison.
author: @halidyer5595

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This was both hard to watch and incredible to watch.  My extreme admiration goes out to you for documenting something so difficult.  People casually talk of "mental health" as this amorphous concept.  Your bravery in putting yourself out there and attaching a face to a facet of it is extraordinary.  There are so many different struggles people go through.  It's painful seeing someone having to live like that on a daily basis.  I hope you realize just how much of a good you're doing by showing everyone that you can put one foot in front of the other, and that there is no stigma, only a struggle that people have to face down regularly, which takes a lot of strength.  Thank you so much for what you show us all.
author: @revcliff

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Spent 40+ years in and out of psychiatric hospitals until we discovered I had a biological problem, not a psychological one. Changed my diet and changed my life. Been mentally free for over 10 years now.
author: @66sprint

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Sleep is so important for any patient.   We've known that forever. Sadly, hospitals in the U.S.  haven't figured that out.  You are very articulate.  Unfortunately, from what I have heard, there is far from enough activities for psychiatric patients.  Patients sitting around is  typical for psychiatric patients.  .
author: @monaoconnell5650

title: PULSTAR nuclear reactor core at NC State University.
body:
We always made fun our reactor.

Visitors often ask: “what does it power?”

Tbh, enough power to heat up my coffee. Its about the size of a storage closet.
author: @rufus2028

title: PULSTAR nuclear reactor core at NC State University.
body: I stood over the pool at Limerick Generating station during a maintenance shutdown. The water does look extremely inviting !!!!

title: PULSTAR nuclear reactor core at NC State University.
body: The really scary/amazing thing to me is that I remember standing right where the camera is and looking into the same pool when I took a NE class at NCSU, almost 40 years ago.

And now i've moved out of that godforsaken room for 2nd sem 😅.

title: 6AM COLLEGE DAY IN MY LIFE 🍂 (productive, studying for finals, fall semester at USC)
body: that girl gave good tips for waking up early, put on music and start your routine. my mouthwash is in before i sit on the toilet sometimes to kick things off! a heater in the bathroom if its cold will also really help keep you from running back into your warm bed! also, idc if im already awake, that alarm sound infuriates me. i wouldve gone in and said “please shut off your alarm its been going off and woke me up” AS LOUDLY AS POSSIBLE, standing over her. if you havent already, i highly recommend “the 5AM Club” by Robin Sharma, there are some helpful gems in there!

title: Touring Every Florida College So You Don't Have To | (UF UM UCF FAU ETC)
body: I go to FGCU and I absolutely LOVE the school. I wish he talked more about the water and nature because that is what the whole campus revolves around. And that waterfront is literally the best because you can rent out boats, canoes, kayaks, paddle boards, etc. as well as a beautiful resort style pool and aquatic center. The place truly looks like a resort and it blows me away every day that I get to study there.

title: Touring Every Florida College So You Don't Have To | (UF UM UCF FAU ETC)
body: Love being an Owl alumni! It’s a completely different campus than when I was there, there was no engineering building, they hadn’t built the gym and new pool until my senior year, and we still had to drive to some random high school stadium 30 minutes away because the stadium hadn’t been built. The rapid development of that campus has been amazing to watch, and I love getting tickets that have access to the full service bar IN THE STADIUM! How awesome is that?!

body: USF is in a shady area. But, at the same time, the campus is awesome, there’s a bunch of natural areas right outside of campus, and a lot of cool places on campus if you like nature. Also, river walk, ybor, st.Pete, Clearwater and Sarasota are pretty close and have so many fun things to do for relatively cheap.

body: As a high school senior, who is currently looking Florida colleges, I toured UF and wasn’t as impressed by it as a lot of people are. The campus wasn’t really my vibe and everything felt very serious. I actually much preferred USF and UCF campuses and environments so much so that I didn’t end up applying to UF, but it was really interesting to see your takes on the campuses!
